items.headline: Global Tensions Drive Down Markets; S&P 500 Records Third Consecutive Weekly Decline
items.summary: U.S. equities closed lower on Friday, with all major indices extending their weekly losses as escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly the Iran war and its impact on oil prices, fueled investor caution. The S&P 500 marked its first three-week losing streak in roughly a year, hitting a new low for 2026. Volatility and trading volume increased significantly, suggesting broad-based selling rather than sector rotation.

items.headline: Sable Offshore Corp. Leads March Gains
items.summary: Sable Offshore Corp. ($SOC) has been a significant gainer, leading the March 2026 rankings with a substantial monthly increase.

items.headline: Oracle Jumps on Strong Earnings and Guidance
items.summary: Oracle ($ORCL) saw its shares jump approximately 9% earlier this week following robust fiscal third-quarter results and optimistic guidance that surpassed Wall Street expectations.

items.headline: HPE Rises After Beating EPS Estimates
items.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise ($HPE) shares rose 3% after reporting first-quarter fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings per share of $0.65, exceeding the $0.59 estimate, driven by a 151% boost in networking revenue.

items.headline: Ulta Beauty Plunges on Weak Forecasts
items.summary: Ulta Beauty ($ULTA) shares sank 14.24% today, making it a top S&P 500 decliner, after the company issued soft full-year profit and comparable sales forecasts.

items.headline: Adobe Slumps Following CEO Departure News and Growth Concerns
items.summary: Adobe ($ADBE) slumped 7.58% today after the announcement that its CEO plans to step down after 18 years, coupled with concerns over slowed annual recurring revenue growth.

items.headline: Dollar General Slides on Disappointing Guidance
items.summary: Dollar General ($DG) shares slid 9% today despite beating Q4 earnings estimates, as investors were reportedly disappointed by the company's short- and long-term guidance.

items.headline: Technology and Materials Lead Market Declines, Utilities and Staples Show Resilience
items.summary: Today's trading saw the Technology and Materials sectors as the leading laggards, dropping 1.29% and 1.04% respectively. Conversely, Utilities and Consumer Staples led the gainers, advancing 0.94% and 0.54%, indicating a defensive shift in the market. The Financial sector remains the worst-performing S&P 500 group year-to-date, pulling back approximately 11%.

items.headline: Fed Poised to Hold Rates Steady Next Week Amid Geopolitical Headwinds
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is widely expected to maintain current interest rates at its upcoming policy meeting on March 17-18. Market participants will closely monitor the Fed's new policy statement, Chair Jerome Powell's press conference, and updated economic projections for insights into how policymakers assess the impact of the ongoing Iran war and rising oil prices on inflation, jobs, and growth.

items.headline: Economic Data Points to Slowdown: PCE Rises, GDP Revised Down, Consumer Sentiment Falls
items.summary: Recent economic data reflects a mixed but concerning picture. The Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index rose 0.3% month-over-month in January, with the core PCE up 0.4%. However, the BEA revised fourth-quarter real GDP growth down to a mere 0.7% from 1.4%, signaling a sharper economic slowdown in late 2025. Adding to the caution, preliminary March consumer sentiment dropped to its lowest level this year, though job openings saw a slight increase in February.

items.headline: Iran War Drives Oil Prices Above $100, Fuels Inflationary Fears
items.summary: Escalating tensions from the Iran war, particularly the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, continue to exert significant pressure on global energy markets. Crude oil futures have seen historic increases, with Brent crude soaring 11% over the week to settle at $103.14 per barrel, intensifying concerns about sustained inflation and its impact on the global economy.

items.headline: AI Rally Continues to Drive Nasdaq Amid Broader Market Weakness
items.summary: Despite broader market weakness, the artificial intelligence (AI) rally, spearheaded by companies like Nvidia ($NVDA), remains a critical driver for the Nasdaq and overall US stock market. Nvidia reported robust fourth-quarter financial results with revenue increasing 73%, and analysts forecast significant upside for technology and consumer discretionary sectors, which collectively account for 80% of the Nasdaq's performance.
